title: Grace Yang

infobox.value: ([simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 罗昭容; [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 羅昭容; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Luó Zhāoróng*) is a Taiwanese [statistician](/wiki/Statistician "Statistician") whose research areas include [stochastic processes](/wiki/Stochastic_process "Stochastic process") in the [physical sciences](/wiki/Outline_of_physical_science "Outline of physical science"), [asymptotic theory](/wiki/Asymptotic_theory_(statistics) "Asymptotic theory (statistics)"), and [survival analysis](/wiki/Survival_analysis "Survival analysis"). She is a professor of [statistics](/wiki/Statistics "Statistics") in the department of [mathematics](/wiki/Mathematics "Mathematics") at the [University of Maryland, College Park](/wiki/University_of_Maryland,_College_Park "University of Maryland, College Park"). She was president of the International Chinese Statistical Association for 1990–1991 and program director for statistics at the [National Science Foundation](/wiki/National_Science_Foundation "National Science Foundation") from 2005 to 2008.[[1]](#cite_note-profile-1)[[2]](#cite_note-nist-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Chinese statistician

**Grace Lo Yang** ([simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 罗昭容; [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 羅昭容; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Luó Zhāoróng*) is a Taiwanese [statistician](/wiki/Statistician "Statistician") whose research areas include [stochastic processes](/wiki/Stochastic_process "Stochastic process") in the [physical sciences](/wiki/Outline_of_physical_science "Outline of physical science"), [asymptotic theory](/wiki/Asymptotic_theory_(statistics) "Asymptotic theory (statistics)"), and [survival analysis](/wiki/Survival_analysis "Survival analysis"). She is a professor of [statistics](/wiki/Statistics "Statistics") in the department of [mathematics](/wiki/Mathematics "Mathematics") at the [University of Maryland, College Park](/wiki/University_of_Maryland,_College_Park "University of Maryland, College Park"). She was president of the International Chinese Statistical Association for 1990–1991 and program director for statistics at the [National Science Foundation](/wiki/National_Science_Foundation "National Science Foundation") from 2005 to 2008.[[1]](#cite_note-profile-1)[[2]](#cite_note-nist-2)

## Education and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Grace_Yang&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Education and career")]

Yang is originally from China, but moved to Taiwan in 1949.[[2]](#cite_note-nist-2) After doing her undergraduate studies at [National Taiwan University](/wiki/National_Taiwan_University "National Taiwan University"), she completed her doctorate in 1966 from the [University of California, Berkeley](/wiki/University_of_California,_Berkeley "University of California, Berkeley"), under the supervision of [Lucien Le Cam](/wiki/Lucien_Le_Cam "Lucien Le Cam"). Her dissertation was on *Contagion in Stochastic Models for Epidemics*.[[2]](#cite_note-nist-2)[[3]](#cite_note-mgp-3) With Le Cam, she is the author of *Asymptotics in Statistics: Some Basic Concepts* (Springer, 1990; 2nd ed., 2002).[[4]](#cite_note-asymptotics-4)

## Awards and honors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Grace_Yang&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Awards and honors")]

Yang is a member of the [International Statistical Institute](/wiki/International_Statistical_Institute "International Statistical Institute"), and a fellow of the [Institute of Mathematical Statistics](/wiki/Institute_of_Mathematical_Statistics "Institute of Mathematical Statistics").[[1]](#cite_note-profile-1)[[2]](#cite_note-nist-2)
